reviews.review_description:
WONDERS offers 3 immersive experiences with stunning graphics that pay homage to these historical monuments. Technically flawless, its only drawback is the lack of translation into various languages, which would make it accessible even to younger audiences.

An experience not to be missed!

The pyramid section stood out as the strongest part of the experience. The visuals are impressive, the historical facts are engaging, and the lighting inside the tunnels and temple is very well done. There is still room to expand the outside environment, but overall the section delivers a memorable and immersive feel. A treasure-hunt element based on its historical facts would make it even more captivating.

The Taj Mahal and Statue of Unity sections are decent, though they do not reach the same level of impact. The Statue of Unity, in particular, could benefit from additional refinement and visual improvement.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the detailed feedback. We’re pleased to hear that the Pyramids of Giza experience stood out—especially the visuals, lighting, and historical depth. Your idea of a history-based treasure hunt is insightful and aligns with features we’re actively exploring.

We also appreciate your honest input on the Taj Mahal and Statue of Unity. Feedback like yours helps us continuously elevate WONDERS, and we look forward to delivering even more immersive updates ahead.

reviews.review_description:
It was an incredible experience. I really wanted to be able to see the changes in the pyramid compared to the past and present, and to be able to walk inside and walk around. In particular, the part where professional explanations are carried out even in small elements that are easy to pass can be highly appreciated educationally.
Although the graphic does not feel like it is composed of actual photos, it is sufficient to feel realistic because it contains quite detailed parts. It was touching to appreciate the Taj Mahal's detailed pattern of fences.
If language localization progresses, it is sufficiently recommended for educational purposes for children.
I hope additional wonder places will continue to be implemented as DLC in the future.

This is not a very long experience currently, but what's here is very interesting. It gives you a sense of scale for how large the pyramids of Egypt actually are, with the highlight of actually going inside them a couple of them. If you're interested in Egypt or it's history, it's a great experience.

That said, the app itself could use some work. Controls aren't explained at all, so parts of the experience could be missed because of this. It took me a while to figure out that there was a way to teleport for instance, which is basically required as the walking speed is very slow.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your detailed feedback! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the immersive experience and the sense of scale that our app provides when exploring the Pyramids. Bringing history to life and offering unique perspectives are goals we hold dear.

We appreciate your constructive comments about the controls and usability. We're currently working on updates to improve the user experience, including better tutorials and clearer instructions for navigating the app. Your point about the walking speed and teleportation feature is noted, and we'll ensure these aspects are addressed in future updates.

Your support and insights help us refine the app further, so thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts. Stay tuned for exciting updates, and we hope to continue bringing you more engaging historical explorations in the future!

reviews.review_title: Great concept and the execution is close
reviews.review_description:
I didn't expect a lot going in, so I was pretty impressed.  That said, I think there's some things that could be better.

The app and controls are fairly easy to use and I had mostly no problem getting around though I found myself wanting more control in the fly-around portion.  The graphics are good for a learning app.  I'd be blown away if they were photo-realistic renderings, which I think is feasible to do, but understand why that extra mile isn't needed for this app.  The NPC seems to use AI and Whisper (or similar) to allow you to ask it questions, but had a hard time with questions I had that were on topic.  If there's not a RAG employed there, it's something to consider, maybe.

I learned quite a bit while using the app in a non-boring way and I'd love to see the other wonders and even things like the human skeletal example (on their website) or full layered human biology lesson.

Great launch offering here and I'm anxious to see what's next for it.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review! We're glad you found the app easy to use and educational. We appreciate your feedback on the controls and graphics, and we'll consider your suggestions for improvements. Enhancing our AI responses is also a priority.

We're excited about your ideas for future content and appreciate your support. Stay tuned for more updates!

reviews.review_description: I have visited the pyramids in real life and this brought me back to that experience.  I particularly enjoyed seeing the pyramid in various modes (X-Ray, time travel), and walking inside. In spite of the simple rendering style, I found the experience very immersive, thanks to the fire torch and its dynamic lighting. The UI, the writing, and the voice overs are also great. I easily get motion sickness but did not get it in the walkthrough. Maybe a bit in the flyover as the camera rotates on its own. The birds and the camels are a nice touch.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your detailed and positive review! We're thrilled to hear that our app provided you with a captivating and immersive experience reminiscent of visiting the pyramids in real life. We're glad you enjoyed exploring the various modes and walking inside the pyramid, and that the dynamic lighting added to the immersion. We'll definitely take your feedback about motion sickness into consideration for future updates. We appreciate your support and hope you continue to enjoy our app!

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed the little experience of Egyption history. The ability to see what is believed to be the original forms of the pyramids and then time travel to what they are like now is a good comparison, however the Sphinx just does not look to line up the same. 

There are a lot of things that need some work, like the settings not being available, a bit of tutorial or guide on what the controls do, some options on movement control like speed, smooth turn and height. Some of the voice lines pronounce things oddly like “28ft” being eff-tee and the AI NPC wasn’t so obvious you need to talk to him. Also would be nice if he was in other walkthrough areas and at least would give the same answers to the tidbits you already have available, like the paint on the Sphinx he says there is no evidence of. Also there are some areas you can’t teleport to, but can walk over and the other way around. Those tidbits of info would also be nice to continue talking and not start over in their small little spot. 

Overall, it is a good start for something that can be very educational and informative. It needs some more polish and refinement to make it better, but gets the point across.

developer_response.body:
hank you for your feedback! We're glad you enjoyed the historical experience and the comparison between past and present pyramids. Your suggestions for improvements, including settings access, controls tutorial, movement options, and AI NPC interactions, are very helpful. We'll work on these issues to enhance the app.

We appreciate your support and look forward to making the app even better!

reviews.review_description: I have downloaded the app and was excited to try it. It loads and then asks me to login. I cant see where I need to create an account. Can you help?

developer_response.body: Thank you for your interest in our app! We're currently in the development phase, and creating an account isn't available directly within the app yet. However, if you'd like to try it out, please email us at business@phibonacci.com, and we'll provide you with the login credentials. We appreciate your patience and support as we work on improving the user experience.

reviews.review_description: I never played a game like this before and I never been to the pyramids. So for this game to make me feel like I'm actually there and being able to explore them on a ground level is great. I loved the visuals, and the art style of the characters. It's cool and it's still in app labs so I'm excited to see what's coming in the future. It's a great way to learn about the pyramids and the sphinx. It's like a full body history lesson that's fun.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your wonderful review! 🌟 We're thrilled to hear that you had an exciting and immersive experience exploring the pyramids and the Sphinx through our game. Our team put a lot of effort into the visuals and art style, and it’s fantastic to know that you loved them. Your excitement for future updates means a lot to us, and we’re dedicated to bringing even more engaging content your way. Stay tuned for more adventures and educational experiences. Happy exploring! 🚀

reviews.review_description: Beautiful app, very well maintained. Very interesting to discover architectural works of ancient Egypt. Keep up the good works!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the wonderful review! We're thrilled that you find our app beautiful and well-maintained. It's fantastic to hear that you're enjoying discovering the architectural works of ancient Egypt. We appreciate your support, and we'll certainly keep up the good work!
